jokes.text: All in favor of the knapsack filled with canned goods, chainsaw, gasoline and emergency radio in case he wakes up post-apocalypse.
jokes.setup: Group is deciding on appropriate gift for someone in a coma
jokes.punchline: Escalates to apocalypse survival kit as if coma patient will wake up to end times
jokes.explanation: Strong absurdist escalation that takes mundane hospital gift discussion to ridiculous extreme with specific survival items

jokes.text: Nope. Baskets have it.
jokes.setup: After elaborate apocalypse survival kit suggestion
jokes.punchline: Deadpan dismissal back to boring chocolate baskets
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation after absurd buildup, economical and well-timed

jokes.text: So as it turns out, unless you're a young child, or a prison inmate, you don't need anyone supervising you.
jokes.setup: Office functioning without a manager
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comparison to children and prisoners needing supervision
jokes.explanation: Classic Jim observational humor with good sarcastic edge, well-structured

jokes.text: Must be because the stakes are so high.
jokes.setup: Observing that office somehow works without supervision
jokes.punchline: Deadpan sarcasm about the low-stakes nature of paper sales
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim delivery, good economy and character voice

jokes.text: Dwight Schrute. Yes, I would. DWIGHT: Thank you. Jordan, gather my things from my desk.
jokes.setup: Jo asks if Dwight wants to be acting manager
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately assumes full authority and starts giving orders
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight character moment - immediate power grab with typical over-confidence

jokes.text: Wait.
jokes.setup: Dwight immediately assuming manager role
jokes.punchline: Jim's horrified realization of what just happened
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction beat, perfect economy

jokes.text: What have you done?
jokes.setup: Continuation of Jim's horror at Dwight's promotion
jokes.punchline: Escalated despair at the situation
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Jim's reaction, maintains comedic momentum

jokes.text: You'd slow me down.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if they get to go with Dwight in some fantasy scenario
jokes.punchline: Dwight's blunt, practical dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight logic - brutally honest and practical even in fantasy

jokes.text: No, a week. Just feels like three months.
jokes.setup: Dwight claiming he's been acting manager for three months
jokes.punchline: Jim's correction revealing it's only been a week but feels eternal
jokes.explanation: Excellent setup/punchline structure showing how unbearable Dwight's management is

jokes.text: Let's see. We all have to punch into a time clock, which is very old, very strong, and has the slot about the size of a finger.
jokes.setup: Describing Dwight's management changes
jokes.punchline: Ominous description of time clock that sounds like medieval torture device
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy implied by description, suggests Dwight's authoritarian tendencies

jokes.text: We were all given new business cards, big enough to set us apart from the competition. Which is how I learned that our titles are all now junior employee.
jokes.setup: New business cards to set them apart from competition
jokes.punchline: Cards reveal everyone has been demoted to 'junior employee'
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sounds like promotion but reveals mass demotion, very Dwight

jokes.text: Mine's at 10:30 and I find that the first hour of the day goes by a lot quicker than the second seven hours.
jokes.setup: Describing staggered lunch breaks
jokes.punchline: Absurdly early lunch time making day feel endless
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist math that perfectly captures workplace misery under Dwight

jokes.text: ALL: I pledge allegiance to the Flag of the United States of America and to the Republic for which it stands, one nation under God, indivisible, with liberty and justice for all.
jokes.setup: Dwight making office recite pledge
jokes.punchline: Absurd sight of office workers doing pledge of allegiance
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight authoritarianism taken to absurd extreme

jokes.text: Your copier code is a distinct 21-digit number that is unique to you and you only, okay? Don't share it.
jokes.setup: Dwight giving morning announcements
jokes.punchline: Absurdly complex security for basic office copier
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight over-complication of simple office task

jokes.text: Question. If we've already fomented insurrection, may we be grandfathered in?
jokes.setup: Dwight warning against fomenting insurrection
jokes.punchline: Jim's mock-serious question about grandfathering existing insurrection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim moment - uses bureaucratic language to mock Dwight's authority, perfect character voice

jokes.text: Define 'foment.' You define 'foment.'
jokes.setup: Dwight asks Jim to define foment
jokes.punchline: Jim turns it back on Dwight, revealing neither knows the word
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing both characters' stubbornness and vocabulary limits

jokes.text: Ah-ha! Oh, my God! What is wrong with you? Got you!
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining new color-coding system
jokes.punchline: Dwight startles Jim with sudden outburst
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy bit, fits Dwight's character but not particularly original

jokes.text: It's none of your business, actually. It's very medical and personal.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking why someone is late
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about medical condition to avoid punishment
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor from oversharing personal information

jokes.text: You stop me when I reach the diseased area.
jokes.setup: Employee claiming medical condition
jokes.punchline: Dwight's inappropriate response to examine them
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight inappropriate response, good cringe factor

jokes.text: In fairness, Darryl did tell me that joke, so... Oh! Darryl's funny.
jokes.setup: Andy deflecting credit for joke
jokes.punchline: Awkward attempt to give credit creates more awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Minor awkward moment, typical Andy overthinking

jokes.text: Andy, do you like being alone with me right now? No, this is horrifying.
jokes.setup: Gabe asking if Andy likes being alone with him
jokes.punchline: Andy's immediate horrified response
jokes.explanation: Excellent cringe comedy - Gabe's neediness met with brutal honesty

jokes.text: No, I don't like being alone with me, either. Okay?
jokes.setup: Andy's horrified response to being alone with Gabe
jokes.punchline: Gabe's self-aware admission of his own unpleasantness
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Gabe's self-loathing adds depth to his patheticness

jokes.text: No, just give me a second. I don't want anyone to know I've been crying.
jokes.setup: Andy wanting to leave uncomfortable conversation
jokes.punchline: Gabe admitting he's been crying, extending the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good cringe continuation, shows Gabe's emotional fragility

jokes.text: Like the French Revolution or The Black Panthers or communism.
jokes.setup: Kevin saying The Fist is just a social club
jokes.punchline: Compares it to historically violent movements
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kevin logic - completely undermines his 'innocent social club' claim

jokes.text: God, I hate when everybody calls us a rebellion.
jokes.setup: Just compared their club to violent revolutionary movements
jokes.punchline: Complains about being called a rebellion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin obliviousness - immediate contradiction of his own words

jokes.text: Unfortunately, it's a bad day, what with Operation Overthrow and everything.
jokes.setup: Claiming it's just a social club
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions 'Operation Overthrow'
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Kevin continues to undermine his own cover story with increasingly obvious names

jokes.text: You're gonna love it. It's Beyonce falling with a fart mixed in.
jokes.setup: Kevin wanting to show Pam a video
jokes.punchline: Describes juvenile video mashup with childish enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfectly Kevin-level humor - juvenile but authentic to character

jokes.text: I will never be happier than I am right now. I will also never be less happy. I will be at my current maximum happiness for the rest of my life.
jokes.setup: Creed as acting manager
jokes.punchline: Bizarre philosophical statement about happiness plateau
jokes.explanation: Peak Creed weirdness - existential philosophy delivered with complete confidence, highly quotable

jokes.text: Acting manager.
jokes.setup: Creed's grandiose happiness speech
jokes.punchline: Deflating correction of his title
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic deflation of Creed's moment

jokes.text: You mean the frozen mice for the piranha? No, the blueberry slurpy pouch. Hmm. He means the ice pack.
jokes.setup: Discussion of snacks Dwight put in freezer
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly disturbing contents of office freezer
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how bizarre office has become under Dwight

jokes.text: I don't believe in horoscopes. I don't believe in Christmas. I sure as hell don't believe in God.
jokes.setup: Gabe professing love to Erin
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly serious things he doesn't believe in
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Gabe's pretentious nihilism during romantic gesture

jokes.text: Or it could be. It's possible that that is exactly what God is.
jokes.setup: Just denied belief in traditional God
jokes.punchline: Immediately backtracks in rambling uncertainty
jokes.explanation: Shows Gabe's intellectual insecurity undermining his confident proclamation

jokes.text: Do we not have voicemail? Dwight doesn't trust robots to give us our messages.
jokes.setup: Phone keeps ringing during Gabe's speech
jokes.punchline: Reveals Dwight's paranoid reason for no voicemail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight paranoia - distrusts basic office technology as 'robots'

jokes.text: but it was a challenge being touched by him.
jokes.setup: Erin describing Gabe as having wonderful qualities
jokes.punchline: Admits physical contact with him was challenging
jokes.explanation: Brutal but polite rejection, perfect Erin diplomacy with devastating honesty

jokes.text: Wow, you've really embraced the whole Bond villain aesthetic.
jokes.setup: Dwight's office setup with gun and piranha
jokes.punchline: Jim's observation about the Bond villain look
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim observation connecting Dwight's power trip to pop culture

jokes.text: The desk is a replica of Uday Hussein's desk. I saw a picture in Newsweek.
jokes.setup: Explaining his office setup
jokes.punchline: Casually admits copying a dictator's son's desk
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark Dwight moment - casual reference to war criminal as interior design inspiration

jokes.text: I promised other people that I'd be on my worst behavior. And I gave them my word, so...
jokes.setup: Dwight asking Jim to promise to be on best behavior
jokes.punchline: Jim claims he already promised others to be on worst behavior
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jim logic - uses honor/integrity to justify being difficult

jokes.text: Don't make me pre-fire you. You wouldn't dare. Watch this. You're pre-fired. And when I'm promoted, you'll be full-fired.
jokes.setup: Dwight threatening to fire Jim despite lack of authority
jokes.punchline: Creates 'pre-firing' concept with escalating threat levels
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation creating new terminology for Dwight's powerless threats

jokes.text: And if you haven't fallen in love with me by then.
jokes.setup: Threatening Jim with full firing when promoted
jokes.punchline: Bizarre romantic condition added to threat
jokes.explanation: Peak Dwight absurdity - adds romantic escape clause to firing threat

jokes.text: But I always say, 'It's better to be pre-pre-pre-prepared.'
jokes.setup: Jim talking about pre-quitting
jokes.punchline: Escalates Dwight's 'pre-' terminology to absurd degree
jokes.explanation: Good callback that escalates the pre-prefix joke to absurd levels

jokes.text: As your pale skin and severe bone structure imply, you'll age swiftly and poorly.
jokes.setup: Dwight telling Jordan people think she was hired for looks
jokes.punchline: Brutal analysis of her future aging process
jokes.explanation: Perfectly brutal Dwight assessment - combines pseudo-science with cruelty

jokes.text: What a day. What a day.
jokes.setup: Dwight adjusting his gun holster
jokes.punchline: Self-satisfied comment while clearly enjoying his power fantasy
jokes.explanation: Visual comedy of Dwight's self-satisfaction with his authoritarian setup

jokes.text: I don't know. I guess he's saying that he's proud of me.
jokes.setup: Asked about why he's carrying a gun
jokes.punchline: Interprets gun gift as sign of family pride
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dwight's need for validation through weapons

jokes.text: Why do you need to keep wearing those booby shirts all the time?
jokes.setup: Asked why he needs to wear holster
jokes.punchline: Deflects with inappropriate comment about women's clothing
jokes.explanation: Typical Dwight inappropriate deflection, fits character but somewhat crude

jokes.text: Why would I put a banana in my holster? In case you weren't hungry now, but you got hungry later.
jokes.setup: Suggestion to put banana in holster instead of gun
jokes.punchline: Logical explanation for banana storage that misses the point
jokes.explanation: Good absurdist logic that both makes sense and completely misses the point

jokes.text: There is a hardwood floor underneath this carpeting. Why would they do that?
jokes.setup: Gun accident reveals floor underneath carpet
jokes.punchline: Bafflement at the illogical construction decision
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about pointless office construction

jokes.text: No, that's not true. Actually, we heard a loud noise, later a hole was seen. No one saw the bullet leave the gun.
jokes.setup: Someone saying Dwight shot Andy
jokes.punchline: Oscar's overly precise, lawyer-like correction of the facts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Oscar pedantry - technically correct but absurdly precise in crisis

jokes.text: Everybody, Oscar found a reason to look on WebMD.
jokes.setup: Oscar offering to check Andy's symptoms on WebMD
jokes.punchline: Calling out Oscar's hypochondriac tendencies
jokes.explanation: Good character observation about Oscar's medical anxieties

jokes.text: No! We really shouldn't be without a receptionist. Now more than ever.
jokes.setup: Gabe offering to take Andy to hospital
jokes.punchline: Prioritizes reception duties during medical emergency
jokes.explanation: Good Gabe bureaucratic priorities trumping basic human decency

jokes.text: Yee-haw! Woo-hoo! Howdy, partners. It's me, Gun Safety Dwight.
jokes.setup: Dwight trying to address the gun incident
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate cowboy persona for gun safety discussion
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment - Dwight's tone-deaf approach to serious situation

jokes.text: I can't do this.
jokes.setup: Trying to maintain cowboy gun safety persona
jokes.punchline: Immediately breaks character, admitting failure
jokes.explanation: Good comedic deflation - Dwight's rare moment of self-awareness

jokes.text: What hand was I holding the gun in? What did Andy's tie look like? Navy blue, little red anchors. I have no way of knowing if that's true.
jokes.setup: Dwight testing eyewitness accounts
jokes.punchline: Gets specific answer but dismisses it as unverifiable
jokes.explanation: Good comedy of Dwight trying to lawyer his way out of obvious guilt

jokes.text: Managing you for this last week has been the greatest honor of my life. And if you ruin this, I will burn this office to the ground.
jokes.setup: Dwight expressing love for his employees
jokes.punchline: Immediately follows with violent threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight contradiction - heartfelt sentiment immediately undermined by threat

jokes.text: I love you guys, but don't cross me, but you're the best.
jokes.setup: Continuing his conflicted message
jokes.punchline: Rapid alternation between love and threats
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Dwight's emotional confusion between affection and authority

jokes.text: I don't feel good about it, but he just kept calling himself a gunshot victim, and it got to me.
jokes.setup: Darryl talking about Andy in hospital waiting room
jokes.punchline: Admits to getting annoyed with Andy's victimhood claims
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Darryl's patience wearing thin with Andy's drama

jokes.text: ? Why did my temporary boss go on a shooting spree? ?
jokes.setup: Andy offering to sing his solo
jokes.punchline: Song about the shooting incident
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy response - turning trauma into performance opportunity

jokes.text: Put everything back in the vending machine, except the fruit.
jokes.setup: Demanding Dwight reverse his policies
jokes.punchline: Specific exception that reveals their priorities
jokes.explanation: Good detail showing office workers' junk food priorities

jokes.text: And when I cough, can you do jazz hands? What's jazz hands? Fine.
jokes.setup: Jim asking for ridiculous favor during Jo's visit
jokes.punchline: Dwight doesn't know what jazz hands are but agrees anyway
jokes.explanation: Good setup for physical comedy with Dwight's ignorance making it funnier

jokes.text: Just like a man, wants to jump right into it while I still got my socks on.
jokes.setup: Dwight wanting to begin their meeting immediately
jokes.punchline: Jo's inappropriate sexual metaphor for business meeting
jokes.explanation: Typical Jo inappropriate comment that makes everyone uncomfortable

jokes.text: Hey, Andy, did you tell Erin about our conversation earlier when you said you would never want to date her again?
jokes.setup: Gabe interrupting Andy and Erin
jokes.punchline: Reveals Andy's private promise in front of Erin
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gabe manipulation - using Andy's promise as weapon in front of Erin

jokes.text: You were in hysterics and my maternal instincts kicked in.
jokes.setup: Andy describing comforting Gabe
jokes.punchline: Describes himself having maternal instincts toward Gabe
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor with Andy's awkward metaphor for helping Gabe

jokes.text: That is between me and my diary.
jokes.setup: Asked about his true feelings for Erin
jokes.punchline: References keeping a diary
jokes.explanation: Good character detail - Andy's diary reference fits his sensitive nature

jokes.text: KEVIN: I don't feel anything. DWIGHT: It's wet. Push harder, Dwight. I can't. I can't push harder.
jokes.setup: Kevin demanding back massage from Dwight
jokes.punchline: Increasingly uncomfortable massage with gross details
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing power dynamics reversed with gross-out elements

jokes.text: But don't eat it.
jokes.setup: Kevin telling Dwight to knead his back like pizza
jokes.punchline: Adds clarification not to eat his back
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin logic - follows pizza metaphor to its absurd conclusion

jokes.text: You've got to admit it, it's nice to have a little power, eh? How's it feel?
jokes.setup: Jo talking to Dwight about management
jokes.punchline: Asks about power just before taking it away
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony as audience knows Jo is about to fire him

jokes.text: Beaumont Adams is a girl's gun. That just makes it plain stupid.
jokes.setup: Dwight mentioning it was a Beaumont Adams gun
jokes.punchline: Jo's dismissive response about it being a 'girl's gun'
jokes.explanation: Good Jo moment showing her gun expertise while insulting Dwight

jokes.text: Stop asking yourself easy questions so you can look like a genius.
jokes.setup: Dwight asking himself rhetorical questions about his mistakes
jokes.punchline: Jo calls out his transparent self-questioning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy calling out common rhetorical device, highly quotable

jokes.text: But I will say, in your one week, every single one of the orders went out on time and I think that is shagadelic, baby.
jokes.setup: Jim consoling fired Dwight
jokes.punchline: Works in the 'shagadelic baby' phrase he promised earlier
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback that Jim manages to work in naturally during emotional moment

jokes.text: Could you turn the volume down on the beeping? Shut up, Oscar.
jokes.setup: Someone asking to turn down time clock volume
jokes.punchline: Creed's rude dismissal as acting manager
jokes.explanation: Good Creed moment showing his abrupt management style

jokes.text: I hit three, I hit three instead. God!
jokes.setup: Struggling with complicated time clock
jokes.punchline: Frustration with Dwight's overcomplicated system
jokes.explanation: Callback to Dwight's overcomplicated systems causing ongoing problems
